reference: Hello, it may be entirely a soft tissue injury, or an undisplaced fracture may also be suspected depending on the severity of the trauma. Yellow discoloration may be due to trauma-related onycholysis, where the nail separates from the nail bed. Since throbbing pain is still felt on the third day, it is better to have a physical examination and an X-ray if required, along with ibuprofen, which relieves the pain and edema as well. Hope it helps.
text: Hello, it may be entirely a soft tissue injury or an undisplaced fracture may also be suspected depending on the severity of the trauma. Yellow discoloration may be due to trauma-related onycholysis where the nail separates from the nail bed. Since throbbing pain is still felt on the third day, it is better to have a physical examination and an x-ray if required, along with ibuprofen, which relieves the pain and edema as well. Hope it helps.
